attributes.description: Alerts when any hints are in progress (`cassandra.storage.total_hints.in_progress.count > 0`). In-progress hints indicate replicas were recently unreachable.
attributes.name: [Cassandra OTel] Hints in progress (replicas unreachable)

investigation_guide.blob:
## Cassandra hints in progress

### What fired
`cassandra.storage.total_hints.in_progress.count` exceeded 0 on at least one node.

### Why it matters
When a replica is unreachable, coordinators write hints for it that are replayed once the replica recovers. Hints in progress signal that one or more replicas were recently down or network-partitioned. If hints accumulate faster than they can be replayed, data loss becomes possible after `max_hint_window_in_ms`.

### Triage
1. Overview dashboard: identify nodes generating hints and their destinations.
2. Check gossip / liveness state across the cluster.
3. Correlate with error rate spikes and network health.
4. Confirm the unreachable node has come back online and hint replay is progressing.

### Remediation
- Restore the unreachable node(s) as soon as possible.
- If a replica is permanently lost, decommission and let hinted handoff expire; then run `nodetool repair` to restore consistency.

### Tuning
- Fires on any active hint. In-progress hints are always worth noting — do not loosen.

esqlQuery.esql:
TS metrics-cassandra.otel-default
// Gauge: hints currently being sent; > 0 means replicas are unreachable
| WHERE cassandra.storage.total_hints.in_progress.count IS NOT NULL
| STATS hints_in_progress = MAX(MAX_OVER_TIME(cassandra.storage.total_hints.in_progress.count)) BY server.address
| WHERE hints_in_progress > 0
| SORT hints_in_progress DESC
